title: Add Facial review queue, montage, and export lineage
operator_request: The Facial app is used to sort mined images for LoRA training. Handshake needs review queues, montage/contact-sheet style inspection, and non-destructive export lineage connected to Ingest/CKC.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Review decisions must be attributable and reversible. Exports must write manifests and artifact refs without mutating or deleting source images.

scope.summary: Port Facial review sessions, parallel-safe shard claims, montage mapping, and Kohya/LoRA export lineage into Atelier Ingest.
base_scope: Create review sessions over an Ingest batch or Facial run artifact.
Support pass/reject/unsure decisions with actor attribution, timestamps, reasons, tags, and notes.
Support parallel agent shard claims so multiple models can review without overlapping the same item by accident.
Generate montage/contact-sheet artifacts with tile-to-image mapping and source refs.
Export accepted candidates with a manifest recording source, decision lineage, dedupe state, identity/quality artifacts, and output refs.
Keep originals read-only; export copies or manifests only.
acceptance_criteria: Review sessions use stable content-hash/source-ref image IDs.
Shard claims are disjoint, actor-attributed, expirable/recoverable, and safe for parallel agents.
Decisions persist as receipts and can be replayed into summary status.
Montage artifacts include tile-to-image mappings for Argus/operator inspection.
Export manifest records source candidates, decisions, dedupe/identity/quality artifacts, and exported files without mutating originals.
risks_and_controls: Risk: two agents decide the same item inconsistently. Control: shard claims and decision receipts must include actor and conflict status.
Risk: export loses source lineage. Control: manifest must include source refs, hashes, review receipts, and upstream artifact refs.
Risk: montage tile mapping becomes visually unverifiable. Control: tests must assert tile map and Argus-visible refs.
verification: Non-destructive source fixture test and frontend/Argus state assertion if UI is included.

implemented: Added native Facial review session artifacts with stable candidate IDs based on content hash plus source ref, with source-ref fallback.
Added actor-attributed shard claim receipts with disjoint active claims, expiry checks, and explicit expired-claim recovery.
Added review decision receipts preserving entered pass/reject/unsure vocabulary while normalizing to accept/reject/hold for replay.
Added status replay, Argus-addressable montage tile-map artifacts, and manifest-only non-destructive export lineage.
Updated the Facial native registry and internal UserManual anchors for model/operator recovery.
implementation_summary.important_scope_boundary: This MT implements the backend artifact contract and proof tests. Transactional API/DB persistence and operator-facing/Argus-steerable UI remain carried forward into MT-029 and MT-030; do not claim full runtime queue parity from MT-028 alone.

passed: direct execution of atelier_facial_review_tests binary: 5 passed, 0 failed
direct execution of user_manual_content_tests ckc_posekit_quickstart_is_native_first_and_reachable: 1 passed, 13 filtered
git diff --check on the scoped MT-028 files
static scan found no stale Facial review placeholder strings in touched registry/test paths
static scan found no hardcoded local path leaks in scoped MT-028 files
inconclusive: A filtered cargo lib-test run for facial_native_registry_covers_required_source_families timed out and is not claimed as passing.
The manual filtered cargo invocation timed out before harness completion, but the freshly built manual test binary passed directly.
Full workspace Cargo proof was intentionally skipped under the operator instruction to avoid conflicting with another heavy Cargo run.
known_follow_up: MT-029 must expose command/API/receipt parity over these artifacts with durable persistence and concurrency semantics.
MT-030 must expose the review queue/montage/export flow in the Ingest UI and make it Argus-steerable without foreground focus theft.

pre_implementation_adversarial_review.lens: Handshake integration, parallel claims, lineage, Argus inspectability
addressed: Did not overload review_recommendation as a decision ledger.
Registry strings now describe implemented backend artifact contracts instead of placeholder recommendation state.
Candidate IDs avoid duplicate collapse by hashing content_hash plus source_ref.
Montage output includes explicit tile-to-image mapping and Argus selectors.
Export manifest is non-destructive and replays decision receipts.
pre_implementation_adversarial_review.carried_forward: Transactional API/DB persistence and UI operation are required in MT-029/MT-030.

pre_implementation_adversarial_review.lens: Original Facial app review queue behavior
addressed: Mapped Facial accept/reject/hold to operator pass/reject/unsure while preserving entered vocabulary.
Ported session, claim, decision, status, montage-map, and export-manifest concepts as native Handshake artifacts.
Kept exports manifest-only and lineage-rich rather than mutating source datasets.
pre_implementation_adversarial_review.carried_forward: Original Facial command wrappers and service API parity are MT-029 scope.

review_result.post_implementation_adversarial_review: Integrated locally through targeted tests and static scans; no separate live subagent patch was applied after implementation. Validator should treat API/DB/UI runtime parity as outstanding, not as an MT-028 pass condition.

manual_requirement: Required if this MT exposes or changes operator/model-visible Facial behavior.

handoff.note: Backend artifact core is ready for validation at product commit 25af5486. active_mt_id remains MT-010 because that is the separate active media/linking lane. Continue Facial runtime parity through MT-029 and MT-030 before claiming operator-usable review queue completion.
